proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 77 binary features in ds001_nsclc, at least one binary feature is associated with the rate of objective_response (chi-square p<0.05).
feature_013 is associated with a lower rate of objective_response than its absence (response rate in feature_013=1 is lower than in feature_013=0).
feature_006 is associated with a higher rate of objective_response than its absence (response rate in feature_006=1 is higher than in feature_006=0).

analyses.code: for c in binary_cols: stats.chi2_contingency(pd.crosstab(df[c], df['objective_response']))
analyses.result_summary: Univariate chi-square screen of all 77 binary features against objective_response. 14 features were significant at p<0.05. Top hits: feature_013 (15.4% vs 19.6%, OR=0.74, p=1.1e-33), feature_067 (14.4% vs 17.7%, p=2.0e-18), feature_006 (18.2% vs 16.4%, p=1.0e-06), feature_007 (17.5% vs 16.4%, p=5.9e-04), feature_039 (17.4% vs 16.4%, p=3.4e-03).

proposed_hypotheses.text: At least one of the 35 continuous lab/measurement features is associated with objective_response.
Higher feature_011 is associated with a lower rate of objective_response (negative slope per SD in univariate logistic regression).
Higher feature_099 is associated with a higher rate of objective_response (positive slope per SD).

analyses.code: for c in cont_cols: sm.Logit(y, sm.add_constant(z[c])).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Univariate logistic regression of standardized continuous features. feature_011 strongest negative predictor (beta=-0.149/SD, p=1.8e-32). feature_099 positive predictor (beta=+0.045/SD, p=1.4e-04). feature_063 negative (beta=-0.044/SD, p=6.9e-04). feature_092 positive (beta=+0.035/SD, p=3.2e-03).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_051 (ordinal 0/1/2, likely performance status) is associated with objective_response, with response rate decreasing monotonically as feature_051 increases.
Patient race (feature_123) is associated with response rate; specifically white patients have lower response than non-white patients.
Histology (feature_043: adenocarcinoma vs squamous) is associated with response rate.
Smoking status (feature_057) is associated with response rate.

analyses.result_summary: feature_051 is strongly associated with response (beta=-0.375 per unit, p=1.7e-93). Response rate is 21.1% (PS0, n=17557), 15.7% (PS1, n=24962), 11.1% (PS2, n=7481) - clear monotonic decline.
Race chi-square p=0.045. Rates: white 16.5% (n=32607), hispanic 17.2%, asian 17.7%, black 17.8%, other 18.5%. White is the lowest-responding stratum.
Histology chi-square p=0.078; adenocarcinoma 16.7% (n=36099), squamous 17.4% (n=13901). Trend toward higher response in squamous but not significant.
Smoking chi-square p=0.27; current 17.2%, former 16.8%, never 16.4%. No significant overall effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_011 has a non-linear relationship with objective_response, in particular feature_011=0 (28% of cohort) marks a distinct higher-response group than feature_011>0.
After accounting for the linear effect of feature_011, a quadratic term is statistically significant (suggesting curvature).

analyses.result_summary: feature_011=0 patients (n=14064) have response rate 19.3%; feature_011>0 patients (n=35936) have response rate 16.0%. Decile analysis shows monotonic decrease from 19.2% (0-0.3) to 13.2% (9.4-28). Difference is significant.

Linear term significant (beta=-0.044, p=1.1e-07); quadratic term not significant (beta=+0.0005, p=0.46). Effect is essentially monotone linear in feature_011, with the bulk of structure captured by linear term.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable logistic regression including all features, feature_051 (PS), feature_013, feature_067, feature_011, feature_006, feature_099, feature_063, feature_092 each retain independent effects on objective_response (p<0.01 in adjusted model).
Patient age (feature_078, range 30-90) has no significant association with objective_response after adjusting for other features.

analyses.code: sm.Logit(y, sm.add_constant(X_all)).fit()  # all binary, ordinal, continuous, dummied categorical
analyses.result_summary: Full multivariable logit (~120 columns) converged. Strongest independent effects (signed beta, p): feature_051 -0.382 (p=8e-96), feature_013 -0.303 (p=1e-34), feature_011 -0.153/SD (p=2e-33), feature_067 -0.261 (p=3e-19), feature_006 +0.131 (p=2e-06), feature_099 +0.047/SD (p=1e-04), feature_063 -0.046/SD (p=5e-04), feature_007 +0.081 (p=9e-04), feature_039 +0.078 (p=1e-03), feature_092 +0.037/SD (p=2e-03). 14 covariates significant at p<0.05.
feature_078 (likely age, mean 65, SD 10) has no association with response (beta=+0.005/SD, p=0.67). Decile-stratified rates 16.4-17.3% with no monotonic trend. Age is not predictive in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The three positive-effect binary features feature_006, feature_007, and feature_039 show a synergistic positive pairwise interaction with one another (interaction beta>0, p<0.05) on objective_response.
feature_013 and feature_067 (both negative effects) act independently and additively (no significant 2-way interaction).

analyses.result_summary: feature_006 x feature_039 interaction beta=+0.184, p=4.2e-04. feature_007 x feature_039 beta=+0.146, p=2.4e-03. feature_006 x feature_007 beta=+0.149, p=4.3e-03. All three pairwise interactions among the positive-effect trio are positive and significant.
feature_013 x feature_067 interaction beta=+0.039, p=0.51. The two negative features act independently; no statistical evidence for synergy or antagonism between them.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a statistically significant 3-way interaction among feature_006, feature_007, and feature_039: response rate when all three are positive is higher than additive expectations from main effects + 2-way interactions.
When all three of feature_006/007/039 are simultaneously positive, the response rate is at least 5 percentage points higher than in any other combination of these three features.

analyses.result_summary: 3-way interaction (006*007*039) beta=+0.391, p=1.8e-04 in unadjusted model and beta=+0.418, p=7.3e-05 in adjusted model. Raw response rates by triplet score (sum 006+007+039): 0->16.1%, 1->16.4%, 2->16.8%, 3->22.9%. The all-three-positive group (n=3216, 6.4% of cohort) is the only stratum with elevated response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The 3-way triplet (006*007*039) effect is consistent across histology strata (no significant 4-way triplet x squamous interaction).
The triplet effect is consistent across performance-status strata.
The triplet effect is consistent across smoking strata.

analyses.code: sm.Logit(y, sm.add_constant([trip, sq, trip*sq, plus 092 terms])).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Triplet x squamous interaction beta=+0.046, p=0.61. Adeno: triplet 22.5% vs no-triplet 16.5% (diff=+6.0%). Squamous: triplet 23.9% vs no-triplet 16.8% (diff=+7.1%). Effect sizes are similar across both histologies.
Triplet x feature_051 interaction beta=+0.126, p=0.056 - not significant at alpha=0.05. Triplet effect is broadly similar across PS strata.

Triplet x never-smoker interaction beta=-0.209, p=0.34. In never smokers, triplet response only 19.4% (n=139) vs 16.3% baseline; in current/former, triplet boosts to 23-24%. Underpowered with only 139 never-smoker triplet patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of feature_013 on objective_response is modified (amplified) by feature_034: feature_013 has a stronger negative effect when feature_034=1 than when feature_034=0.
Among feature_034=1 patients (n=5038), the absolute response rate when feature_013=0 (no negative biomarker) is the highest of any subgroup defined by feature_013/034 combinations.

analyses.result_summary: feature_013 x feature_034 interaction beta=-0.215, p=7.8e-03. Stratified: in feature_034=0 (n=44962), feature_013 effect beta=-0.274 (p=3e-26). In feature_034=1 (n=5038), feature_013 effect is much stronger beta=-0.489 (p=2e-10). feature_034 amplifies the negative effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The lower response rate in white vs non-white patients is robust to adjustment for clinical features (ECOG, feature_013, feature_067, feature_011, treatment triplet) - i.e., a residual disparity remains.
The race disparity is NOT explained by differential triplet treatment uptake by race.

analyses.code: sm.Logit(y, sm.add_constant([white, ECOG, f013, f067, f011, trip])).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted white-vs-nonwhite beta=-0.066, p=0.0087. The race disparity persists after adjusting for performance status, key prognostic features, tumor burden, and the treatment triplet. White patients have ~1 percentage point lower response rate across virtually all strata.

Triplet prevalence by race: white 6.49%, asian 6.58%, black 6.21%, hispanic 6.37%, other 5.82%. Difference is not significant (chi-square p>0.5). Triplet usage is essentially uniform across races, so disparity is not due to differential treatment access.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The white-vs-nonwhite outcome gap is consistent across clinical strata (ECOG levels, feature_013 levels, triplet treatment) - i.e., it is a uniform shift, not driven by interaction with another feature.
Insurance type (medicaid, private, uninsured vs medicare) is not associated with response after adjusting for race and clinical features.

analyses.code: stratified analysis: response rate by white/nonwhite within each subgroup
analyses.result_summary: White-vs-nonwhite differences by ECOG (PS0: -1.2%, PS1: -0.7%, PS2: -1.1%), by feature_013 (=0: -0.6%, =1: -1.2%), by triplet (=0: -1.0%, =1: -1.0%). The disparity is remarkably uniform, ~1 percentage point lower across every clinical stratum tested.

analyses.code: sm.Logit(y, sm.add_constant([medicaid, private, white, ECOG, f013, f067, trip])).fit()
analyses.result_summary: After adjusting for race and clinical: medicaid beta=-0.063, p=0.091; private beta=+0.026, p=0.32. Insurance has no statistically significant adjusted effect. Marginal medicaid trend toward lower response is not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Continuous-continuous interactions exist: feature_063 x feature_092 has a significant interaction term in their effect on response (beta>0).
feature_011 (tumor burden) does not modify the effects of other continuous biomarkers (no significant pairwise interactions).

analyses.code: Pairwise interaction tests across top 6 continuous features
analyses.result_summary: feature_063 x feature_092 interaction beta=+0.027/SD2, p=0.040. Other pairwise interactions are non-significant (smallest other p=0.088). The 063x092 interaction is the only significant cont-cont interaction tested but is borderline.

analyses.code: Pairwise tests of feature_011 with each other top continuous
analyses.result_summary: feature_011 interactions with feature_044, feature_092, feature_028, feature_099, feature_063 all p>0.37. feature_011 acts independently (additively) with other continuous features.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_118 (continuous) interacts positively with feature_051 (ECOG): higher feature_118 partially attenuates the negative ECOG effect.
Higher feature_054 interacts negatively with feature_051: in worse-PS patients, higher feature_054 is more harmful than in good-PS patients.

analyses.result_summary: feature_118 x feature_051 interaction beta=+0.059/(SD*PS unit), p=0.0013. Significant interaction; higher feature_118 attenuates ECOG penalty.

feature_054 x feature_051 interaction beta=-0.048, p=0.0081. Significant negative interaction. Combined with feature_070 x ECOG (beta=+0.045, p=0.012) and feature_096 x ECOG (beta=+0.042, p=0.025), several continuous features modify the ECOG effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_092 (continuous, range 0-0.8) interacts strongly and positively with the 006*007*039 triplet on objective_response: response to triplet is much higher when feature_092 is high.
When feature_092 < 0.5 (lower 78% of cohort), the triplet has essentially no effect on response.
When feature_092 >= 0.5, the triplet roughly triples the response rate.

analyses.result_summary: Triplet x feature_092 interaction beta=+0.594/SD, p=7.8e-38 (unadjusted) and beta=+0.604/SD, p=3.0e-38 (adjusted for ECOG, f013, f067, f011, f099, f063). Very large, highly significant biomarker-treatment interaction.
In feature_092 < 0.5 (n=39251): triplet beta=+0.019, p=0.73. Response rates: triplet 0 -> 16.5% (n=36747), triplet 1 -> 16.8% (n=2504). No triplet benefit.
In feature_092 >= 0.5 (n=10749): triplet beta=+1.424, p=8.9e-71. Response rates: triplet 0 -> 16.3% (n=10037), triplet 1 -> 44.8% (n=712). Absolute increase of ~28.5 percentage points.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_092 has a dose-response relationship with triplet benefit: triplet effect grows monotonically with feature_092 decile.
When feature_092 >= 0.6, response rate to triplet exceeds 60%.

analyses.code: Decile-stratified response rates of triplet vs no-triplet across feature_092 deciles
analyses.result_summary: By feature_092 decile, triplet vs no-triplet response: D1: 17.5% vs 17.0%; D2-D7: differences within +/-2pp; D8 (0.512-0.603): 21.1% vs 16.7%; D9 (0.603-0.798): 70.8% vs 16.2% - dose-response is sharply non-linear, consistent with a threshold effect around feature_092 ~0.5-0.6.
Triplet x (feature_092>=0.6) interaction beta=+2.50, p=1.9e-77. In feature_092>=0.6 (n=5165): triplet 0 -> 16.2%, triplet 1 -> 71.0% (n=352). Effect size is ~55 percentage point absolute boost.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Each individual binary among feature_006/007/039 also shows a positive interaction with feature_092 (beta>0, p<0.001), indicating each component contributes to the biomarker-driven response.
No other binary feature in the dataset shows a feature_092 interaction that approaches the effect size of the triplet's interaction.

analyses.result_summary: feature_006 x f092 beta=+0.126, p=1.2e-06; feature_007 x f092 beta=+0.128, p=7.9e-08; feature_039 x f092 beta=+0.125, p=1.6e-07. All three components individually show large positive interactions with feature_092, consistent with a treatment regimen whose components share the PD-L1-like biomarker dependency.

analyses.code: Sweep of all binary features x feature_092 interaction tests
analyses.result_summary: Among 70+ other binaries tested, top non-triplet hits with feature_092: feature_115 (beta=+0.072, p=0.003), feature_109 (-0.081, p=0.023), feature_035 (+0.079, p=0.028). Effect sizes are <15% of the triplet interaction (beta=+0.594). Triplet x feature_092 dominates all alternatives.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The triplet x feature_092 interaction is similar in magnitude in adenocarcinoma and squamous histology (no significant 3-way trip x sq x f092 interaction).
After accounting for triplet x feature_092, there is no residual triplet x squamous interaction.

analyses.code: sm.Logit(y, sm.add_constant([trip, sq, f092, trip*sq, trip*f092, sq*f092, trip*sq*f092])).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Three-way trip x sq x f092 interaction beta=-0.060, p=0.54. Within feature_092>=0.5, response rates are similar across histologies: adeno triplet 44.4% vs none 16.2%; squamous triplet 45.5% vs none 16.8%. Biomarker-driven effect is histology-agnostic.
When trip x f092 (beta=+0.595, p=7e-38) is included, the trip x sq term is non-significant (beta=+0.039, p=0.68). Histology does not modify the treatment-biomarker interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_011 (tumor burden) effect persists similarly in patients receiving the triplet vs not (similar slope per SD).
feature_013 effect on response is consistent across histology strata (similar absolute reduction in response rate).

analyses.code: Stratified univariate logit of feature_011 within triplet=0 vs =1
analyses.result_summary: In triplet=0 (n=46784): feature_011 beta=-0.151/SD, p=1.4e-30. In triplet=1 (n=3216): feature_011 beta=-0.136/SD, p=1.8e-03. Effects are very similar across treatment strata; tumor-burden effect is independent of triplet treatment.
In adeno: feature_013=0 19.5% vs =1 15.1% (diff -4.4pp). In squamous: feature_013=0 19.9% vs =1 16.0% (diff -3.9pp). The effect size is essentially identical across histologies.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_007 (prevalence ~45%) is independent of smoking status, age, and ECOG, suggesting it is a demographic/biological feature like sex rather than a clinical state.
feature_007 has a positive main effect on response only when combined with feature_006 and feature_039 (the triplet); it has minimal effect on its own once adjusted for other covariates.

analyses.code: Cross-tabs of feature_007 with smoking, age, ECOG, race
analyses.result_summary: feature_007 prevalence is 44.6% in current smokers, 44.9% in former, 44.4% in never; 45.6% in <55, 44.7% in >74; identical across races (44-45%). Independent of all clinical states - consistent with sex.

analyses.code: Adjusted multivariable model with main effects + triplet
analyses.result_summary: In adjusted model with the triplet term, feature_007 main effect beta=+0.022, p=0.40 (NS). feature_006 main effect p=0.22, feature_039 main effect p=0.38. None of the three components have a significant main effect once the triplet is included; the entire signal is in the 3-way joint presence.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In never smokers (n=7459), feature_092 has a different relationship with response than in ever smokers - specifically, no positive main effect.
feature_013 and feature_067 are negative predictors of response in never smokers (sign consistent with the full cohort).

analyses.result_summary: In never smokers, high-feature_092 (>=0.5) is associated with LOWER response (beta=-0.177, p=0.026). Among triplet recipients in never smokers (n=34), response is 38.2%, which is lower than in ever smokers (~45%). Suggests biology in never smokers differs (likely driver-mutation-driven), and PD-L1-style biomarker is less relevant.

feature_013 in never smokers: beta=-0.242, p=1.6e-04. feature_067 beta=-0.238, p=1.6e-03. Same sign and similar magnitude as full cohort. These two negative predictors generalize across smoking strata.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The race effect (white vs nonwhite, beta~-0.07) is not modified by the triplet treatment - i.e., no triplet x race interaction.
Among continuous features, only feature_118 shows a marginal interaction with white race (p<0.05).

analyses.result_summary: Triplet x white interaction beta=+0.014, p=0.88. Triplet boost is identical in absolute terms in both racial groups (~+1pp without triplet, ~+1pp within triplet recipients).

analyses.code: Sweep of continuous x white interaction tests
analyses.result_summary: feature_118 x white beta=-0.054, p=0.030; feature_082 x white beta=-0.049, p=0.050. All other continuous x white interactions p>0.07. Magnitudes are small; the race effect is mostly a uniform shift.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_063 negative effect on response is robust to multivariable adjustment for treatment and other prognostic factors.
feature_099 (likely albumin-like, range 1.7-5.5) has a small but significant positive effect on response that is robust to adjustment.

analyses.result_summary: feature_063 adjusted beta=-0.046/SD, p=4.5e-04. Effect retains direction and significance after adjusting for all top features. Magnitude is modest (~1pp absolute change per SD).
feature_099 adjusted beta=+0.047/SD, p=1.0e-04. Patients in higher feature_099 quintiles have ~2pp absolute increase in response (e.g., D1 16.0% vs D10 17.6%). Robust to adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A final adjusted model containing feature_051, feature_013, feature_067, feature_011, feature_063, feature_099, triplet, triplet*feature_092, white, and squamous captures the dominant predictors of objective_response, with all listed terms (except squamous) significant at p<0.01.

analyses.result_summary: Final model: feature_051 beta=-0.382 (p=2e-95); triplet*feature_092 beta=+0.606 (p=2e-38); feature_013 beta=-0.305 (p=5e-35); feature_011 beta=-0.153 (p=1e-33); feature_067 beta=-0.260 (p=5e-19); triplet beta=+0.321 (p=2e-11); feature_099 beta=+0.047 (p=1e-04); feature_063 beta=-0.046 (p=4e-04); white beta=-0.069 (p=7e-03); squamous beta=+0.038 (p=0.16, NS). All non-histology terms retain independent significance.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The clinical-best-case scenario (PS0, no feature_013, no feature_067, on triplet, high feature_092 (z=+1.5)) corresponds to a predicted response rate exceeding 50%.
The clinical-worst-case scenario (PS2, both negative features, no triplet, low feature_092) corresponds to a predicted response rate below 10%.

analyses.code: Predict from final model at scenario covariate vectors
analyses.result_summary: Best-case predicted response: 53.7%. Mid-case (PS0, no neg, no triplet, high f092): 25.4%. Worst-case (PS2, both neg, no triplet, low f092): 8.5%. Predictions span ~45pp range across observable scenarios, mainly driven by feature_051, feature_013/067, and triplet*feature_092.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across the comprehensive screen and interaction battery, the dominant predictors of objective_response in this NSCLC cohort are: (1) performance status (feature_051), (2) tumor burden (feature_011), (3) two negative biomarkers/states (feature_013, feature_067), (4) a 3-component treatment regimen (feature_006/007/039) whose efficacy is essentially gated by a continuous biomarker (feature_092). Demographic disparities (lower response in white patients) persist after clinical adjustment.

analyses.code: Synthesis of all prior iterations - see analysis_summary.txt
analyses.result_summary: Overall conclusions: (a) feature_051 (PS) is the strongest single predictor (p<1e-95). (b) feature_011 (tumor burden, with a meaningful zero stratum) is the strongest continuous predictor (p<1e-33). (c) feature_013/067 are two independent negative predictors. (d) The 3-way triplet 006*007*039 is a textbook biomarker-gated treatment: in feature_092 >=0.5 it triples response (16% to 45%); in feature_092 <0.5 it has no effect. (e) feature_034 amplifies the negative feature_013 effect. (f) White patients show a small but robust ~1pp residual response disparity vs nonwhite patients across all clinical strata, not explained by triplet access. (g) Age, smoking, insurance, and histology have no significant independent effect after adjustment.
